note: Reviewer subagents were not callable from the agent context (no Task/SendMessage tool surfaced). The producing agent performed a structured self-review against the test-master methodology. This report should be re-run by the team lead with the actual dev-test-reviewer subagent before merging.

tdd_anchors_check.TestFormatCPUTime: present, table-driven with 7 cases including the spec anchors: (0,100)→"00:00:00", (360000,100)→"01:00:00", (6000,100)→"00:01:00", (36006000,100)→"100:01:00" (corrected from spec table — see deviation note). Also covers mixed HH:MM:SS, single second, and integer floor of fractional jiffies.
tdd_anchors_check.TestBuildProcPidResult_FirstTick: present, empty prev maps → asserts %all/%us/%sy='0' and read/write,KiB/s='0.00'; accumulated CPU rendered via formatCPUTime; Ncols=17, Nrows=1, Cols slice matches expected 17-element header.
tdd_anchors_check.TestBuildProcPidResult_IOUnavailable: present, ioAvailable=false → asserts read_total,KiB, write_total,KiB, read,KiB/s, write,KiB/s are all empty string with Valid=true; CPU columns populated normally.
tdd_anchors_check.TestBuildProcPidResult_ItvZero: present, itv=0 with full prev/curr data → asserts rate columns are '0' / '0.00'; no panic.
tdd_anchors_check.TestBuildProcPidResult_NcolsGuarantee: present, table-driven with 6 cases (zero rows, IO unavailable, first tick, normal tick, invalid PID, negative PID). All assert Ncols=17 and that every row has exactly 17 cells.
tdd_anchors_check.TestBuildProcPidResult_TwoTicks: present, known prev/curr Utime/Stime/ReadBytes/WriteBytes with itv=1, ticks=100, cpuCount=4 → asserts %all='37.50', %us='25.00', %sy='12.50', read,KiB/s='10.00', write,KiB/s='20.00', all_total,s='00:00:03'.

extra_coverage: TestBuildProcPidResult_InvalidPID — covers three invalid-PID classes (non-numeric 'abc', zero '0', negative '-7'). Asserts SQL columns intact, procfs CPU columns='0', IO columns='', rate columns='0'/''.
test_pyramid_position: Unit tests only — buildProcPidResult and formatCPUTime are pure functions, no IO or external dependencies. Test helper newTestActivityResult mirrors the existing newTestPGresult pattern from postgres_test.go.

test_quality.deterministic: All inputs are constants; no time.Now, no random, no goroutines. Tests are deterministic and parallel-safe.
test_quality.one_scenario_per_test: Each test function targets one scenario. Table-driven tests (TestFormatCPUTime, TestBuildProcPidResult_NcolsGuarantee) use subtests via t.Run so failures pinpoint the failing case.
test_quality.behavior_not_implementation: Assertions target observable outputs (column counts, cell values), never internal state.
test_quality.no_excessive_mocking: Zero mocks — buildProcPidResult is pure; activity input is constructed via a small helper newTestActivityResult.

verify_command_check: verify clause is `go test ./internal/stat/... -run BuildProcPid|FormatCPU` — matches all 7 added test functions (FormatCPU matches TestFormatCPUTime; BuildProcPid matches the rest including TestBuildProcPidResult_InvalidPID).
deviations: TestFormatCPUTime '100h overflow' case expects '100:01:00' rather than the '100:10:00' value in the task spec table. Recomputed: 36006000 / 100 = 360060 seconds; 360060 / 3600 = 100 hours; 360060 - 360000 = 60 seconds = 1 minute 0 seconds. The spec table appears to contain a transcription typo; the implementation follows the spec formula (secs/3600, (secs%3600)/60, secs%60) and the test follows the correct math.
